question: How do collapse threshold (Jcrit) and injection strategy shape the topology of emergent structure in the SOL graph?
hypotheses: Low Jcrit (5–10) produces many stars quickly, creating a 'rich' cosmology with many synth nodes
High Jcrit (30–50) produces few or no collapses, creating a 'barren' cosmology where energy dissipates
Blast injection creates a single hot spot that collapses first and radiates structure outward
Drizzle injection delays collapse but distributes stellar formation across the graph
Cluster spray with spirit nodes produces multiple simultaneous collapses, weaving a synth-web
falsifiers: Stars form at all Jcrit values regardless of threshold (would indicate Jcrit has no discriminating power)
No synth nodes are ever spawned (would indicate the spawning mechanism is broken)
Drizzle and blast produce identical star counts (would indicate injection rate is irrelevant)
notes: Based on rd1.md 'Telekinetic Build' v3.5 → v3.5.1: Jeans Instability (metabolism), Tractor Beam (telekinesis), Active Inference (synth spawning). The two knobs are the density trigger and the energy delivery method.
